key_insight: VFEAgent automates finite element analysis end-to-end from images and text by combining multimodal reasoning with verification-first code generation.
engineering_relevance: The framework can reduce manual modeling effort and make FEA more accessible for engineering design workflows that require fast, reliable simulation.
method: Multimodal vision-language multi-agent pipeline parses heterogeneous inputs into structured FEA specifications.
ReAct-style reasoning guides task decomposition and decision-making across agents.
Verification-first code synthesis adds self-debugging and fallback mechanisms to ensure executable, physically valid simulations.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluated VFEAgent across multiple engineering mechanics scenarios and report higher success in generating complete, physically valid simulations than LLM-based baselines.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on the quality of input images/descriptions and robust handling of edge cases, so human review may still be needed for safety-critical analyses.
paper_title: VFEAgent: A Multimodal Agent Framework for End-to-End Automated Finite Element Analysis
